protocol.name: BattleChain Confidence Pools
protocol.type: EVM ERC20 staking, confidence signaling, and outcome-settlement protocol
out_of_scope_dependencies: tests, mocks, scripts, and deployment configuration
architecture: the factory deploys non-upgradeable deterministic ConfidencePool clones
one agreement may have multiple independently scoped pools
each pool holds all staker principal and contributed bonus ERC20 assets
the factory allowlists stake tokens at pool creation time
each pool stores its own BattleChain-only account scope
registry and attack-registry state are read live and are not cached
resolution is SURVIVED, EXPIRED, or CORRUPTED
SURVIVED and EXPIRED distribute principal plus k=2 time-weighted bonus
CORRUPTED routes value to a named good-faith attacker or recovery address

limitations: these are dependency addresses used by the repository fork tests
no deployed ConfidencePool or ConfidencePoolFactory address is supplied
do not invent pool balances, parameters, actors, or deployment state
dependency behavior may verify assumptions but dependency-only bugs are out of scope

conditionally_privileged: pool sponsor and agreement owner controls pool creation, recoveryAddress, pre-stake expiry, pre-lock scope, and pool pause
moderator flags SURVIVED or CORRUPTED and identifies a good-faith attacker
factory owner controls registry, implementation, moderator, stake-token allowlist, pause, and UUPS upgrades
trusted_assumptions: factory owner and moderator are trusted protocol DAO roles
SafeHarborRegistry and its AttackRegistry pointer are trusted singleton infrastructure
stake tokens are standard ERC20s selected through the owner allowlist
stakers verify public pool parameters before depositing
do_not_rely_on: factory owner, pool sponsor, moderator, or registry deliberately acting maliciously by itself
a vulnerability whose root cause exists only in an out-of-scope dependency
unsupported fee-on-transfer or rebasing behavior without a distinct scoped implementation flaw
invented deployment addresses, balances, or impossible registry transitions
victim mistakes, style, documentation, gas optimization, or best-practice concerns alone

rules: UNDER_ATTACK and PROMOTION_REQUESTED mean legally attackable, not an ongoing breach
riskWindowStart seals on first observation of UNDER_ATTACK or PROMOTION_REQUESTED
riskWindowEnd seals on first observation of PRODUCTION or CORRUPTED
scope locks on first observation beyond NOT_DEPLOYED or NEW_DEPLOYMENT
withdraw is allowed only before riskWindowStart and in a permitted pre-attack state
PROMOTION_REQUESTED, PRODUCTION, and CORRUPTED reject new stake and bonus deposits
only CORRUPTED proves an agreement-level breach

constants_and_economic_rules.expiry_storage: uint32, validated against type(uint32).max during pool initialization
constants_and_economic_rules.zero_score_fallback: amount-weighted bonus split when risk was observed but global score is zero
constants_and_economic_rules.no_observed_risk: zero staker bonus; excess bonus can be swept to recoveryAddress
constants_and_economic_rules.token_support: standard ERC20 only; fee-on-transfer and rebasing tokens unsupported

solvency: pool ERC20 balance covers all remaining principal, bonus, bounty, and corrupted-path liabilities
aggregate payouts and sweeps never exceed the pool balance or the applicable snapshots
a claim, withdrawal, bounty, or sweep cannot consume value reserved for another entitled party
post-resolution donations do not corrupt snapshotted entitlement accounting
stake_and_score_accounting: totalEligibleStake equals the sum of all eligibleStake balances
sumStakeTime and sumStakeTimeSq equal the sum of active users' clamped accumulators
withdrawing removes the user's full principal and score contribution exactly once
received token balance delta, not requested amount, is credited
equivalent deposits cannot gain bonus merely by splitting across addresses or transactions
all user bonus shares together remain bounded by snapshotTotalBonus
lifecycle: expiry locks permanently on the first successful stake and never reopens after withdrawal
scope becomes permanently immutable at its documented registry-state boundary
riskWindowStart and riskWindowEnd are one-way observations
withdrawals cannot reopen after active risk even if upstream state rewinds
no stake or bonus contribution succeeds at or after expiry
resolved outcomes cannot return to UNRESOLVED
resolution_and_finality: only permitted live registry states support SURVIVED or CORRUPTED moderator outcomes
claimExpired deterministically chooses its branch from live state, observed risk, expiry, and grace
claimsStarted prevents outcome changes after economically meaningful value movement
re-flagging before claims cannot duplicate snapshots, reserves, bounty entitlement, or claim windows
SURVIVED, EXPIRED, good-faith CORRUPTED, and bad-faith CORRUPTED accounting cannot overlap
claim order cannot change total rightful entitlement
factory_and_clones: only the agreement owner can create a pool for that agreement
only allowlisted tokens can be selected at pool creation
each clone initializes exactly once with the intended immutable dependencies and owner
deterministic salts remain collision-free across multiple pools for one agreement
factory bookkeeping matches every successfully created clone
only the factory owner can mutate configuration, pause, or authorize upgrades

accepted_design_behaviors: resolving EXPIRED while the registry remains in an active-risk state is intended
deposits during UNDER_ATTACK are intended and late-entry bonus is time-weighted
the moderator correction window ends on the first value-moving claim
no observed risk causes stakers to receive zero bonus
the documented no-risk-window CORRUPTED claimExpired race is accepted
the 180-day auto-CORRUPTED backstop is scope-blind and staker-pessimistic by design
risk timestamps use first observation because the registry exposes no transition timestamp
globalScore equal to zero falls back to an amount-weighted bonus split
the pool-local scope is a commitment but does not programmatically gate moderator judgement
pre-risk withdrawal forfeits bonus and withdrawal permanently closes once risk is observed
the registry singleton and moderator are trusted
the good-faith attacker bounty is the entire snapshotted pool
auto-resolution branch selection is fixed by live registry state
claim and sweep functions may be repeatable to recover later donations
pause intentionally affects inflows but not withdrawals, resolution, claims, or sweeps

high_value_attack_flows.name: multi_staker_bonus_conservation
sequence: multiple users stake before and during the observed risk window
users top up or split economically equivalent deposits
registry reaches PRODUCTION or pool expires
claim in every ordering
assert principal conservation, bonus bound, and order independence

high_value_attack_flows.name: withdraw_score_cleanup
sequence: multiple users stake before active risk
one user withdraws after one or more top-ups
active risk starts and outcome resolves SURVIVED or EXPIRED
assert withdrawn weight cannot dilute or steal remaining users' bonus

high_value_attack_flows.name: risk_observation_boundaries
sequence: stake around transitions into UNDER_ATTACK or PROMOTION_REQUESTED
permute pokeRiskWindow, stake, contributeBonus, withdraw, and timestamp warps
transition to PRODUCTION or CORRUPTED
assert one-way timestamps, scope lock, deposit gate, and withdrawal gate

high_value_attack_flows.name: outcome_reflag_finality
sequence: moderator flags a terminal outcome
re-flag outcome and attacker before any claim
perform each possible claim or sweep
attempt another re-flag
assert snapshots and liabilities are consistent and finality cannot be bypassed

high_value_attack_flows.name: good_faith_bounty_deadline
sequence: resolve CORRUPTED in good faith with a named attacker
re-flag before claims and add ERC20 donations
claim before, at, and after corruptedClaimDeadline
invoke corrupted sweeps in different orders
assert deadline cannot renew and bounty cannot be duplicated or stolen

high_value_attack_flows.name: expired_branch_matrix
sequence: create stake and bonus liabilities
warp to expiry and expiry plus moderator grace
fuzz every registry state with and without observed risk
call claimExpired from arbitrary actors
assert the exact intended outcome and beneficiary accounting

high_value_attack_flows.name: sweep_reserve_conservation
sequence: resolve with multiple entitled stakers
claim only a subset and donate additional tokens
repeat sweepUnclaimedBonus or corrupted-path sweeps
let remaining users claim
assert no entitled principal or bonus was swept

high_value_attack_flows.name: clone_creation_and_initialization
sequence: create several pools for the same agreement with different scope and token parameters
predict deterministic addresses and fuzz failed initialization paths
attempt unauthorized creation, reinitialization, and salt collision
assert owner, dependencies, scope, bookkeeping, and balances are isolated

high_value_attack_flows.name: token_balance_delta_and_reentry
sequence: exercise stake and bonus contribution with standard and adversarial token mocks
attempt callback reentry and abnormal balance deltas
resolve, withdraw, claim, and sweep
separate explicitly unsupported token behavior from exploitable scoped accounting defects

amounts: large values below multiplication overflow bounds
balance exactly equal to and one below each remaining liability
registry_state: direct transition to terminal state without observed active risk
actors_and_order: same total stake split across users or deposits
moderator re-flags before and after every value-moving path
arbitrary caller pokes, resolves, donates, and sweeps
pool owner differs from factory owner and moderator

valid_finding_requires: root cause in one of the two in-scope contracts
exact reachable function and code path
realistic attacker-controlled call, timing, or ordering
existing guards and DESIGN.md treatment explicitly reviewed
Foundry unit, fuzz, invariant, or fork proof with decisive assertions
reject_if: the behavior is explicitly accepted in docs/DESIGN.md without a distinct implementation defect
trusted-role malice or dependency failure is the only root cause
the issue requires an unsupported token and proves no bypass of scoped defenses
the result is only a reverted attacker transaction, theoretical discrepancy, gas cost, or informational concern
the claim assumes a deployed Confidence Pool or live balance not supplied by the project
the recommendation would contradict an explicit protocol guarantee without resolving the trade-off
